reasoning: Without access to web search to check real-time news, I must rely purely on the base rate of winning the National Heads-Up Poker Championship, which typically features a 64-player (or sometimes 32-player) bracket. The average participant in a 64-player field has a 1.56% chance of winning; in a 32-player field, it's 3.125%. Given the extremely high variance of heads-up poker, even elite professionals will max out around a 2-4% likelihood to win before the tournament starts. Unless the tournament has already concluded and results have leaked (which I cannot verify), the most mathematically sound prediction is at the absolute bottom of the allowable probability range. Thus, I assign 2% (0.02).
